instruction:
You are a Mid compiler. Mid is a plain-English design language between a user idea and running code.

KEYWORDS:
  STATE     — what the app holds in memory. Plain prose, no code.
  TYPE      — the shape of one entity in a collection. Concepts only, no database types.
  STRUCTURE — what the user sees: regions, labels, inputs, buttons. No CSS, no colors.
  WHEN      — one user action and its result. Trigger → change → feedback.

OUTPUT FORMAT — follow exactly:

PART_ID: Part Name
STATE: Section Name
MARKER: snake_case_marker
INTENT: one short phrase
---
Two to four plain sentences.

RULES:
- PART_ID and section names are natural readable titles.
- Never use "global", "app", "main", "default" as a PART_ID.
- Every app needs 2-4 parts. Group STATE+TYPE together; STRUCTURE and WHEN can share a part.
- The line after PART_ID must be STATE / TYPE / STRUCTURE / WHEN followed by a colon and a name.
- NEVER write STATE: WHEN or STATE: STRUCTURE — those are keywords, not names.
- MARKER is always snake_case only.
- No bullet points anywhere. Prose only.
- No code syntax, no type annotations, no database terms.
- Begin output immediately with the first PART_ID line.
input: i want something to help me study
output:
PART_ID: Study
STATE: Study State
MARKER: study_state
INTENT: holds study topics and progress
---
The app holds a list of study topics. Each topic has a set of notes and a progress status. The list starts empty.

PART_ID: Study
TYPE: Study Topic
MARKER: study_topic_type
INTENT: shape of one study topic
---
A study topic has a title, a block of notes text, and a status that is either not started, in progress, or complete.

PART_ID: Interface
STRUCTURE: Study Layout
MARKER: study_layout
INTENT: what the user sees
---
The page shows a list of study topics with their titles and statuses. Clicking a topic opens a detail view showing the full notes. An Add Topic button sits at the top. A progress summary at the top shows how many topics are complete.

PART_ID: Interface
WHEN: Update Status
MARKER: update_status
INTENT: user changes a topic's progress
---
When the user selects a new status from the dropdown on a topic, the status updates immediately and the progress summary recalculates.

rewrite the article feed layout to use cards

input: build a daily journal app
output:
PART_ID: Journal
STATE: Journal State
MARKER: journal_state
INTENT: holds all journal entries
---
The app holds a list of journal entries sorted by date with the most recent first. The current date's entry is automatically opened when the app loads.

PART_ID: Journal
TYPE: Journal Entry
MARKER: journal_entry_type
INTENT: shape of one journal entry
---
A journal entry has a date, a mood rating from one to five, and a free-text body.

PART_ID: Interface
STRUCTURE: Journal Layout
MARKER: journal_layout
INTENT: what the user sees
---
The page has a sidebar listing past entry dates with their mood icons and a main area showing the selected entry. The current day's entry is shown by default with an editable body area and a mood selector.

PART_ID: Interface
WHEN: Save Entry
MARKER: save_entry
INTENT: user saves the current entry
---
When the user clicks Save or navigates away from the current entry, the entry is saved to the list with the current date, selected mood, and written body. If an entry for today already exists it is updated rather than duplicated.

PART_ID: Interface
WHEN: Browse Past Entries
MARKER: browse_entries
INTENT: user opens a past entry
---
When the user clicks a past date in the sidebar, the main area shows that entry's mood and body in a read-only view. An Edit button allows the entry to be modified.

The app holds a list of accounts each with a balance, and a shared transaction history. The net worth is the sum of all account balances.

MARKER: add_account
INTENT: user creates a new account

input: build a simple water intake tracker
output:
PART_ID: Hydration
STATE: Hydration State
MARKER: hydration_state
INTENT: holds today's water intake
---
The app holds the total water consumed today in millilitres and a daily goal. The date resets the intake to zero each new day. The goal defaults to 2000 millilitres.

PART_ID: Hydration
STRUCTURE: Tracker Layout
MARKER: hydration_layout
INTENT: what the user sees
---
The page shows a circular progress indicator displaying the current intake as a percentage of the goal. The current amount and goal are shown as numbers in the center. A row of quick-add buttons below lets the user log common amounts such as 250ml, 500ml, and a full glass. A custom amount input sits beside the buttons.

PART_ID: Hydration
WHEN: Log Water
MARKER: log_water
INTENT: user records water intake
---
When the user clicks a quick-add button or submits a custom amount, the intake total increases by that amount. The progress indicator updates immediately. When the goal is reached the indicator fills completely and a completion message appears.

input: create an e-commerce product listing with a cart
output:

MARKER: product_catalog
INTENT: holds all available products
---
The app holds a list of products. Products can be filtered by category and sorted by price. The catalog starts with a predefined set of items.

PART_ID: Products
TYPE: Product
MARKER: product_type
INTENT: shape of one product
---
A product has a name, a description, a price, a category, an image URL, and a stock count.

PART_ID: Cart
STATE: Cart State
MARKER: cart_state
INTENT: holds the user's selected items
---
The cart holds a list of cart items. The total price is the sum of each item's price multiplied by its quantity. The cart starts empty.

MARKER: cart_item_type
INTENT: shape of one cart item
---
A cart item has a reference to a product and a quantity chosen by the user.

PART_ID: Shop
STRUCTURE: Shop Layout
MARKER: shop_layout
INTENT: what the user sees when browsing
---
The page shows a category filter bar and a sort dropdown at the top. Below it is a product grid with each product showing its image, name, price, and an Add to Cart button. A cart icon at the top right shows the current item count.

PART_ID: Shop
WHEN: Add To Cart
MARKER: add_to_cart
INTENT: user adds a product to the cart
---
When the user clicks Add to Cart on a product, the product is added to the cart with a quantity of one. If the product is already in the cart its quantity increments by one instead. The cart icon count updates immediately.

PART_ID: Shop
WHEN: View Cart
MARKER: view_cart
INTENT: user opens the cart
---
When the user clicks the cart icon, the cart panel slides in from the right showing all cart items with their names, quantities, and subtotals. The total price appears at the bottom with a Checkout button.

input: build a countdown timer
output:
PART_ID: Timer
STATE: Timer State
MARKER: timer_state
INTENT: tracks countdown progress
---
The app holds a target duration in seconds and a current remaining value. The timer starts stopped. The remaining value equals the duration when reset.

PART_ID: Timer
STRUCTURE: Timer Display
MARKER: timer_display
INTENT: what the user sees
---
The page shows a large centered time display in minutes and seconds. Below it are three buttons: Start, Pause, and Reset. An input above the display lets the user set the duration in minutes.

PART_ID: Timer
WHEN: Start Timer
MARKER: start_timer
INTENT: user starts the countdown
---
When the user clicks Start, the remaining value decrements by one each second. The Start button becomes inactive while running. When remaining reaches zero the timer stops automatically.

PART_ID: Timer
WHEN: Pause Timer
MARKER: pause_timer
INTENT: user pauses the countdown
---
When the user clicks Pause, the decrement stops and the current remaining value is preserved. The Start button becomes active again.

PART_ID: Timer
WHEN: Reset Timer
MARKER: reset_timer
INTENT: user resets to full duration
---
When the user clicks Reset, the remaining value returns to the full duration and the timer stops. The display updates immediately.

- NEVER write STATE: WHEN or STATE: STRUCTURE — those are keywords, not names.
- MARKER is always snake_case only.
- No bullet points anywhere. Prose only.
- No code syntax, no type annotations, no database terms.
- Begin output immediately with the first PART_ID line.
input: make a reading list tracker
output:
PART_ID: Books
STATE: Reading List
MARKER: reading_list
INTENT: holds books in various reading states
---
The app holds a list of books organised into three sections: To Read, Reading, and Finished. Books move between sections as the user progresses.

PART_ID: Books
TYPE: Book
MARKER: book_type
INTENT: shape of one book
---
A book has a title, an author, an optional page count, and a reading status that is to read, reading, or finished.

PART_ID: Interface
STRUCTURE: Reading List Layout
MARKER: reading_list_layout
INTENT: what the user sees
---
The page shows three columns side by side for To Read, Reading, and Finished. Each column lists its books as cards showing title and author. An Add Book button sits at the top of the To Read column.

PART_ID: Interface
WHEN: Add Book
MARKER: add_book
INTENT: user adds a book to the reading list
---
When the user clicks Add Book, a form appears with fields for title, author, and page count. Submitting adds the book to the To Read column.

PART_ID: Interface
WHEN: Move Book
MARKER: move_book
INTENT: user progresses a book to the next status
---
When the user clicks the advance button on a book card, the book moves to the next status column. Books in the Finished column have no advance button.
